Off Calendar Feb-14-2020 Set For Trial On Jul-20-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on DEFENDANT THERESA BRESKI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Theresa Breski's demurrer to the second cause of action of the complaint is overruled. The complaint adequately pleads each element of an open book account. "The only essential allegations of a common count are '(1) the statement of indebtedness in a certain sum, (2) the consideration, i.e., goods sold, work done, etc., and (3) nonpayment.'" (Farmers Ins. Exchange v. Zerin (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 445, 460; see also Interstate Group Administrators, Inc. v. Cravens, Dargan & Co. (1985) 174 Cal.App.3d 700, 708 ["A book account may furnish the basis for an action on a common account ' . . . when it contains a statement of the debits and credits of the transactions involved completely enough to supply evidence from which it can be reasonably determined what amount is due to the claimant.' [Citations.] A book account is described as 'open' when the debtor has made some payment on the account, leaving a balance due."].) "In California, it has long been settled the allegation of claims using common counts is good against special or general demurrers." (Farmers Ins. Exchange, 53 Cal.App.4th at 460.) Defendant's argument that Plaintiff cannot assert such a claim because it has also brought a claim for breach of express contract is wrong. "'It makes no difference in such a case that the proof shows the original transaction to be an express contract, a contract implied in fact, or a quasi-contract.'" (Utility Audit Co., Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 950, 958.) In any event, a party is permitted to plead in the alternative.

Counsel for the plaintiff is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
